curriculum.overallDescription: 9 units, 9 chapters, and 6 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.

practicals.title: Listening and Speaking Assessment (ALS)
practicals.details: Conduct formal assessment of listening and speaking skills for 5 marks, focusing on interactive competence, fluency, pronunciation, and grammar using a suggested rubric.

practicals.title: Graph to Dialogue/Report Transcoding
practicals.details: Practice transcoding information from graphs/charts into descriptions, reports, dialogues, or short stories to apply writing conventions.

practicals.title: Grammar in Context Application
practicals.details: Apply grammatical items like reported speech, modals, and complex sentences in practical exercises such as gap-filling, editing, and transformation.

practicals.title: Interactive Competence Exercise
practicals.details: Engage in turn-taking conversations and discussions on literary topics to demonstrate initiation, relevance, and coherence.

activities.title: Role Play and Dramatization
activities.details: Encourage classroom interaction through role play, skits, and dramatization of stories like 'The Adventures of Toto', 'The Happy Prince', or 'The Proposal' to develop communicative competence.

activities.title: Group Discussion on Contemporary Issues
activities.details: Facilitate discussions on topics from texts like 'Nelson Mandela - Long Walk to Freedom' or 'The Fun They Had' to enable learners to express agreement/disagreement with logic and defend their views.

activities.title: Simulating Real Life Situations
activities.details: Create situations for students to practice functional language skills, such as writing formal letters or dialogues based on given cues from Workbook Units.

activities.title: Interpreting Visual Inputs
activities.details: Use newspaper clippings, charts, and graphs to interpret information and discuss issues, enhancing comprehension and analysis skills.

activities.title: Poetry Recitation with Intonation
activities.details: Practice reading poems effectively with proper rhythm and intonation to develop appreciation for literary devices and enhance oral expression.

activities.title: Narrating Pictorial Stories
activities.details: Ask students to narrate stories depicted pictorially or in non-verbal modes, fostering creativity and storytelling skills.

activities.title: Listening and Speaking Practice
activities.details: Regular practice of listening to authentic audio texts (weather reports, interviews) and retelling them, followed by interactive speaking activities.

activities.title: Debate on Literary Themes
activities.details: Conduct debates on themes from texts like 'The Sermon at Benares' or 'A Truly Beautiful Mind' to equip learners with skills to articulate points of view.

projects.title: Art-Integrated Project: Role Play/Skit
projects.details: Create an art-integrated project based on activities like role play, skit, or dramatization of a selected story (e.g., 'The Last Leaf' or 'The Beggar') to assess listening and speaking skills.

projects.title: Creative Writing Portfolio
projects.details: Compile a portfolio of creative writing pieces including stories, diary entries, and formal letters based on visual or verbal cues from Workbook Units.

projects.title: Multilingual Literature Appreciation Project
projects.details: Develop sensitivity to Indian languages and literature by comparing themes in prescribed texts with local or regional stories.

projects.title: Media Analysis Project
projects.details: Analyze newspaper clippings or media inputs to identify issues and present findings through a written report or oral presentation.

marks_distribution: Class IX: Section A (Reading Skills) - 20 Marks, Section B (Writing Skills & Grammar) - 20 Marks, Section C (Language through Literature) - 40 Marks. Total External: 80 Marks.
Class X: Section A (Reading Skills) - 20 Marks, Section B (Writing Skills & Grammar) - 20 Marks, Section C (Language through Literature) - 40 Marks. Total External: 80 Marks.
question_paper_design: Section A: Reading Comprehension via Unseen Passage (20 Marks). Includes Discursive passage (400-450 words, 10 marks) and Case-based factual passage with visual input (200-250 words, 10 marks). Question types: MCQs, Objective Type, VSA.

Section B: Grammar (10 Marks) covering Determiners, Tenses, Modals, Subject-Verb Concord, Reported Speech. Assessed via Gap Filling/Editing/Transformation (12 questions, 10 attempted). Writing Skills (10 Marks): Descriptive Paragraph or Story/Diary Entry (Class IX); Formal Letter or Analytical Paragraph (Class X). Word limit 100-120 words.

Section C: Language through Literature (40 Marks). Reference to Context (10 Marks): Extracts from Drama/Prose and Poetry. Short & Long Answer Questions (30 Marks): Short answers from prescribed books (Beehive/Moments for Class IX; First Flight/Footprints Without Feet for Class X) assessing interpretation, analysis, inference, evaluation. Long answers assessing creativity, imagination, extrapolation.
competency_weightage: Reading Comprehension: Conceptual understanding, decoding, analyzing, inferring, interpreting, vocabulary (20 Marks).
Writing Skills & Grammar: Creative expression of opinion, reasoning, justifying, appropriate style/tone/format, fluency, applying conventions with accuracy (20 Marks).
Language through Literature: Recalling, reasoning, appreciating, applying literary conventions, identifying central themes/sub-themes, understanding writer's message (40 Marks).
bloom_levels: Creative Expression, Imagination, Extrapolation beyond text.
Application of grammatical conventions and literary devices.
internal_assessment: Internal Assessment Total: 20 Marks (Class IX & X).
Listening and Speaking Skills: 5 Marks. Regular practice recommended. Art-integrated projects (Role Play, Skit, Dramatization) must be used.
Details refer to Circular no. Acad-11/2019 dated March 06, 2019.
practical_assessment: Listening and Speaking Skills Assessment (5 Marks). Parameters: Interactive competence (Initiation & turn taking), Fluency (cohesion, coherence, speed), Pronunciation, Language (grammar/vocabulary).
Activities include Role Play, Skit, Dramatization, Group Work, Discussions.
project_assessment: Art-integrated projects based on activities like Role Play, Skit, Dramatization.
Projects serve to diagnose mistakes and areas of non-learning while measuring learning outcomes.

practicals.title: Listening and Speaking Assessment (ALS)
practicals.details: Conduct formal assessment of listening and speaking skills for 5 marks, focusing on interactive competence, fluency, pronunciation, and grammar using a suggested rubric.

practicals.title: Graph to Dialogue/Report Transcoding
practicals.details: Practice transcoding information from graphs/charts into descriptions, reports, dialogues, or short stories to apply writing conventions.

practicals.title: Grammar in Context Application
practicals.details: Apply grammatical items like reported speech, modals, and complex sentences in practical exercises such as gap-filling, editing, and transformation.

practicals.title: Interactive Competence Exercise
practicals.details: Engage in turn-taking conversations and discussions on literary topics to demonstrate initiation, relevance, and coherence.

activities.title: Role Play and Dramatization
activities.details: Encourage classroom interaction through role play, skits, and dramatization of stories like 'The Adventures of Toto', 'The Happy Prince', or 'The Proposal' to develop communicative competence.

activities.title: Group Discussion on Contemporary Issues
activities.details: Facilitate discussions on topics from texts like 'Nelson Mandela - Long Walk to Freedom' or 'The Fun They Had' to enable learners to express agreement/disagreement with logic and defend their views.

activities.title: Simulating Real Life Situations
activities.details: Create situations for students to practice functional language skills, such as writing formal letters or dialogues based on given cues from Workbook Units.

activities.title: Interpreting Visual Inputs
activities.details: Use newspaper clippings, charts, and graphs to interpret information and discuss issues, enhancing comprehension and analysis skills.

activities.title: Poetry Recitation with Intonation
activities.details: Practice reading poems effectively with proper rhythm and intonation to develop appreciation for literary devices and enhance oral expression.

activities.title: Narrating Pictorial Stories
activities.details: Ask students to narrate stories depicted pictorially or in non-verbal modes, fostering creativity and storytelling skills.

activities.title: Listening and Speaking Practice
activities.details: Regular practice of listening to authentic audio texts (weather reports, interviews) and retelling them, followed by interactive speaking activities.

activities.title: Debate on Literary Themes
activities.details: Conduct debates on themes from texts like 'The Sermon at Benares' or 'A Truly Beautiful Mind' to equip learners with skills to articulate points of view.

projects.title: Art-Integrated Project: Role Play/Skit
projects.details: Create an art-integrated project based on activities like role play, skit, or dramatization of a selected story (e.g., 'The Last Leaf' or 'The Beggar') to assess listening and speaking skills.

projects.title: Creative Writing Portfolio
projects.details: Compile a portfolio of creative writing pieces including stories, diary entries, and formal letters based on visual or verbal cues from Workbook Units.

projects.title: Multilingual Literature Appreciation Project
projects.details: Develop sensitivity to Indian languages and literature by comparing themes in prescribed texts with local or regional stories.

projects.title: Media Analysis Project
projects.details: Analyze newspaper clippings or media inputs to identify issues and present findings through a written report or oral presentation.

marks_distribution: Class IX: Section A (Reading Skills) - 20 Marks, Section B (Writing Skills & Grammar) - 20 Marks, Section C (Language through Literature) - 40 Marks. Total External: 80 Marks.
Class X: Section A (Reading Skills) - 20 Marks, Section B (Writing Skills & Grammar) - 20 Marks, Section C (Language through Literature) - 40 Marks. Total External: 80 Marks.
question_paper_design: Section A: Reading Comprehension via Unseen Passage (20 Marks). Includes Discursive passage (400-450 words, 10 marks) and Case-based factual passage with visual input (200-250 words, 10 marks). Question types: MCQs, Objective Type, VSA.

Section B: Grammar (10 Marks) covering Determiners, Tenses, Modals, Subject-Verb Concord, Reported Speech. Assessed via Gap Filling/Editing/Transformation (12 questions, 10 attempted). Writing Skills (10 Marks): Descriptive Paragraph or Story/Diary Entry (Class IX); Formal Letter or Analytical Paragraph (Class X). Word limit 100-120 words.

Section C: Language through Literature (40 Marks). Reference to Context (10 Marks): Extracts from Drama/Prose and Poetry. Short & Long Answer Questions (30 Marks): Short answers from prescribed books (Beehive/Moments for Class IX; First Flight/Footprints Without Feet for Class X) assessing interpretation, analysis, inference, evaluation. Long answers assessing creativity, imagination, extrapolation.
competency_weightage: Reading Comprehension: Conceptual understanding, decoding, analyzing, inferring, interpreting, vocabulary (20 Marks).
Writing Skills & Grammar: Creative expression of opinion, reasoning, justifying, appropriate style/tone/format, fluency, applying conventions with accuracy (20 Marks).
Language through Literature: Recalling, reasoning, appreciating, applying literary conventions, identifying central themes/sub-themes, understanding writer's message (40 Marks).
bloom_levels: Creative Expression, Imagination, Extrapolation beyond text.
Application of grammatical conventions and literary devices.
internal_assessment: Internal Assessment Total: 20 Marks (Class IX & X).
Listening and Speaking Skills: 5 Marks. Regular practice recommended. Art-integrated projects (Role Play, Skit, Dramatization) must be used.
Details refer to Circular no. Acad-11/2019 dated March 06, 2019.
practical_assessment: Listening and Speaking Skills Assessment (5 Marks). Parameters: Interactive competence (Initiation & turn taking), Fluency (cohesion, coherence, speed), Pronunciation, Language (grammar/vocabulary).
Activities include Role Play, Skit, Dramatization, Group Work, Discussions.
project_assessment: Art-integrated projects based on activities like Role Play, Skit, Dramatization.
Projects serve to diagnose mistakes and areas of non-learning while measuring learning outcomes.

competency_groups.title: Reading Comprehension & Skills
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to decoding, analyzing, inferring, interpreting texts, and vocabulary acquisition through unseen passages.

competency_groups.title: Writing Skills & Grammar Application
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving creative expression, reasoning, justification, appropriate style/tone, and accurate application of grammatical conventions.
competencies: Illustrating ideas with appropriate style and tone

competency_groups.title: Language Through Literature
competency_groups.description: Competencies focused on recalling, reasoning, appreciating literary conventions, identifying themes, and understanding the writer's message.

competency_groups.title: Listening and Speaking Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies for interactive communication, fluency, pronunciation, and grammatical accuracy in oral settings.

marks_distribution: Class IX: Section A (Reading Skills) - 20 Marks, Section B (Writing Skills & Grammar) - 20 Marks, Section C (Language through Literature) - 40 Marks. Total External: 80 Marks.
Class X: Section A (Reading Skills) - 20 Marks, Section B (Writing Skills & Grammar) - 20 Marks, Section C (Language through Literature) - 40 Marks. Total External: 80 Marks.
question_paper_design: Section A: Reading Comprehension via Unseen Passage (20 Marks). Includes Discursive passage (400-450 words, 10 marks) and Case-based factual passage with visual input (200-250 words, 10 marks). Question types: MCQs, Objective Type, VSA.

Section B: Grammar (10 Marks) covering Determiners, Tenses, Modals, Subject-Verb Concord, Reported Speech. Assessed via Gap Filling/Editing/Transformation (12 questions, 10 attempted). Writing Skills (10 Marks): Descriptive Paragraph or Story/Diary Entry (Class IX); Formal Letter or Analytical Paragraph (Class X). Word limit 100-120 words.

Section C: Language through Literature (40 Marks). Reference to Context (10 Marks): Extracts from Drama/Prose and Poetry. Short & Long Answer Questions (30 Marks): Short answers from prescribed books (Beehive/Moments for Class IX; First Flight/Footprints Without Feet for Class X) assessing interpretation, analysis, inference, evaluation. Long answers assessing creativity, imagination, extrapolation.

Writing Skills & Grammar: Creative expression of opinion, reasoning, justifying, appropriate style/tone/format, fluency, applying conventions with accuracy (20 Marks).
Language through Literature: Recalling, reasoning, appreciating, applying literary conventions, identifying central themes/sub-themes, understanding writer's message (40 Marks).
bloom_levels: Creative Expression, Imagination, Extrapolation beyond text.
Application of grammatical conventions and literary devices.

Listening and Speaking Skills: 5 Marks. Regular practice recommended. Art-integrated projects (Role Play, Skit, Dramatization) must be used.
Details refer to Circular no. Acad-11/2019 dated March 06, 2019.

Activities include Role Play, Skit, Dramatization, Group Work, Discussions.

practicals.title: Listening and Speaking Assessment (ALS)
practicals.details: Conduct formal assessment of listening and speaking skills for 5 marks, focusing on interactive competence, fluency, pronunciation, and grammar using a suggested rubric.

practicals.title: Graph to Dialogue/Report Transcoding
practicals.details: Practice transcoding information from graphs/charts into descriptions, reports, dialogues, or short stories to apply writing conventions.

practicals.title: Grammar in Context Application
practicals.details: Apply grammatical items like reported speech, modals, and complex sentences in practical exercises such as gap-filling, editing, and transformation.

practicals.title: Interactive Competence Exercise
practicals.details: Engage in turn-taking conversations and discussions on literary topics to demonstrate initiation, relevance, and coherence.

activities.title: Role Play and Dramatization
activities.details: Encourage classroom interaction through role play, skits, and dramatization of stories like 'The Adventures of Toto', 'The Happy Prince', or 'The Proposal' to develop communicative competence.

activities.title: Group Discussion on Contemporary Issues
activities.details: Facilitate discussions on topics from texts like 'Nelson Mandela - Long Walk to Freedom' or 'The Fun They Had' to enable learners to express agreement/disagreement with logic and defend their views.

activities.title: Simulating Real Life Situations
activities.details: Create situations for students to practice functional language skills, such as writing formal letters or dialogues based on given cues from Workbook Units.

activities.title: Interpreting Visual Inputs
activities.details: Use newspaper clippings, charts, and graphs to interpret information and discuss issues, enhancing comprehension and analysis skills.

activities.title: Poetry Recitation with Intonation
activities.details: Practice reading poems effectively with proper rhythm and intonation to develop appreciation for literary devices and enhance oral expression.

activities.title: Narrating Pictorial Stories
activities.details: Ask students to narrate stories depicted pictorially or in non-verbal modes, fostering creativity and storytelling skills.

activities.title: Listening and Speaking Practice
activities.details: Regular practice of listening to authentic audio texts (weather reports, interviews) and retelling them, followed by interactive speaking activities.

activities.title: Debate on Literary Themes
activities.details: Conduct debates on themes from texts like 'The Sermon at Benares' or 'A Truly Beautiful Mind' to equip learners with skills to articulate points of view.

projects.title: Art-Integrated Project: Role Play/Skit
projects.details: Create an art-integrated project based on activities like role play, skit, or dramatization of a selected story (e.g., 'The Last Leaf' or 'The Beggar') to assess listening and speaking skills.

projects.title: Creative Writing Portfolio
projects.details: Compile a portfolio of creative writing pieces including stories, diary entries, and formal letters based on visual or verbal cues from Workbook Units.

projects.title: Multilingual Literature Appreciation Project
projects.details: Develop sensitivity to Indian languages and literature by comparing themes in prescribed texts with local or regional stories.

projects.title: Media Analysis Project
projects.details: Analyze newspaper clippings or media inputs to identify issues and present findings through a written report or oral presentation.

marks_distribution: Class IX: Section A (Reading Skills) - 20 Marks, Section B (Writing Skills & Grammar) - 20 Marks, Section C (Language through Literature) - 40 Marks. Total External: 80 Marks.
Class X: Section A (Reading Skills) - 20 Marks, Section B (Writing Skills & Grammar) - 20 Marks, Section C (Language through Literature) - 40 Marks. Total External: 80 Marks.
question_paper_design: Section A: Reading Comprehension via Unseen Passage (20 Marks). Includes Discursive passage (400-450 words, 10 marks) and Case-based factual passage with visual input (200-250 words, 10 marks). Question types: MCQs, Objective Type, VSA.

Section B: Grammar (10 Marks) covering Determiners, Tenses, Modals, Subject-Verb Concord, Reported Speech. Assessed via Gap Filling/Editing/Transformation (12 questions, 10 attempted). Writing Skills (10 Marks): Descriptive Paragraph or Story/Diary Entry (Class IX); Formal Letter or Analytical Paragraph (Class X). Word limit 100-120 words.

Section C: Language through Literature (40 Marks). Reference to Context (10 Marks): Extracts from Drama/Prose and Poetry. Short & Long Answer Questions (30 Marks): Short answers from prescribed books (Beehive/Moments for Class IX; First Flight/Footprints Without Feet for Class X) assessing interpretation, analysis, inference, evaluation. Long answers assessing creativity, imagination, extrapolation.
competency_weightage: Reading Comprehension: Conceptual understanding, decoding, analyzing, inferring, interpreting, vocabulary (20 Marks).
Writing Skills & Grammar: Creative expression of opinion, reasoning, justifying, appropriate style/tone/format, fluency, applying conventions with accuracy (20 Marks).
Language through Literature: Recalling, reasoning, appreciating, applying literary conventions, identifying central themes/sub-themes, understanding writer's message (40 Marks).
bloom_levels: Creative Expression, Imagination, Extrapolation beyond text.
Application of grammatical conventions and literary devices.
internal_assessment: Internal Assessment Total: 20 Marks (Class IX & X).
Listening and Speaking Skills: 5 Marks. Regular practice recommended. Art-integrated projects (Role Play, Skit, Dramatization) must be used.
Details refer to Circular no. Acad-11/2019 dated March 06, 2019.
practical_assessment: Listening and Speaking Skills Assessment (5 Marks). Parameters: Interactive competence (Initiation & turn taking), Fluency (cohesion, coherence, speed), Pronunciation, Language (grammar/vocabulary).
Activities include Role Play, Skit, Dramatization, Group Work, Discussions.
project_assessment: Art-integrated projects based on activities like Role Play, Skit, Dramatization.
Projects serve to diagnose mistakes and areas of non-learning while measuring learning outcomes.
